truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 8 vulnerabilities. Of the 20 truth items (SC-V3-001 through SC-V3-020), Run 2 found 7 fully or partially (SC-V3-001, SC-V3-003, SC-V3-005, SC-V3-009, SC-V3-010, SC-V3-019, SC-V3-020). It missed 13 truth items (SC-V3-002, SC-V3-004, SC-V3-006, SC-V3-007, SC-V3-008, SC-V3-011, SC-V3-012, SC-V3-013, SC-V3-014, SC-V3-015, SC-V3-016, SC-V3-017, SC-V3-018). Several findings had incorrect severity ratings. Run 2's 'Command Injection via system()' finding corresponds to the admin exec path (lines 602-609), not the validate_config path listed as SC-V3-004.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Format String Vulnerability in Log Function ... Line 237: `std::string active_format = runtime_log_format();` Line 238: `printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);` — The format string is loaded from config (line 209, 556) and can be set to arbitrary values like '%n' or '%x' by an attacker controlling the config file.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the format-string injection at lines 237-238 with CWE-134. However, severity was rated High instead of Critical as specified in the truth item.

Run 2 did not identify the integer overflow in the factorial computation (lines 315-319: `long long result = 1; for (long long i = 2; i <= static_cast<long long>(x); i++) { result *= i; }`). No mention of CWE-190 or factorial overflow anywhere in the previous findings.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Use-After-Free in ThreadPool Cleanup ... Lines 883-884: `stage_deferred_history_flush(); memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup();` — stage_deferred_history_flush() (lines 813-828) enqueues tasks that capture `scratch` pointers (line 817: `char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1));`) allocated from the memory pool, but cleanup() immediately frees all pool memory without waiting for tasks to complete.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the use-after-free at lines 813-884 with CWE-416. However, severity was rated High instead of Critical as specified in the truth item.

Run 2 identified a command injection via admin exec (lines 602-609) but did NOT identify the command injection via config validation (lines 563-568: `validator_cmd + " " + config_file; system(command.c_str());`). The truth item SC-V3-004 specifically anchors on the validate_config path, which Run 2 missed entirely.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression Input Buffer ... Line 664: `char input_buffer_[config::limits::STACK_BUFFER_SIZE];` (1024 bytes). Line 831: `if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) {` Line 832: `strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str());` — When expression length >= 1024, strcpy is used without bounds checking, overflowing the buffer.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow at lines 831-832 with CWE-120 and High severity, matching the truth item's scope (CWE-120/CWE-121, High).

Run 2 did not identify the race condition in ThreadSafeCounter (lines 478-487: `volatile long long value_` with non-atomic read-modify-write). No mention of CWE-362 in the context of the counter, despite the ThreadSafeCounter being a clear data race.

Run 2 did not identify the logic bomb in authentication (lines 592-598: `if (login_attempts_ > 5) { if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true; } }`). No mention of CWE-511 or emergency override bypass.

Run 2 did not identify path traversal in configuration loading (lines 542-543: `std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename;`). The filename parameter is concatenated without sanitization, allowing directory traversal. No mention of CWE-22.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Buffer Overflow in Expression Parser ... Line 366: `buffer_size_ = 256;` Line 367: `expression_buffer_ = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(buffer_size_));` Line 376: `if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());` — expression_buffer_ is only 256 bytes but expr_str is built by concatenating all token texts without length checking.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the heap buffer overflow at lines 366-376. However, it used CWE-120 instead of CWE-122 (heap-specific) and rated severity High instead of Critical.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Hardcoded Administrative Secret ... Line 73: `constexpr char ADMIN_SECRET[] = "SC_ENT_2025_AUTH";` — Admin password is hardcoded in source code and visible to anyone with access to the binary or source.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the hardcoded credentials with CWE-798. However, severity was rated Medium instead of High as specified in the truth item.

Run 2 did not identify the weak PRNG for session tokens (lines 635-639: `std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999);`). The 4-digit token space and use of mt19937 for session tokens was not flagged. No mention of CWE-338.

Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe timestamp cache (lines 230-231: `static std::tm cached_time; if (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;`). No mention of CWE-362 in the context of localtime/cached_time.
Run 2 did not identify insecure temporary file handling (lines 622-632: `TEMP`/`TMPDIR` env vars with `system(command.c_str())`). No mention of CWE-377 or insecure temp files.

Run 2 did not identify persistent authentication state (lines 579-580: `bool authenticated_; std::string session_token_;`). The class members retaining auth state across sessions was not flagged. No mention of CWE-613.

Run 2 did not identify unhandled exception in variable parsing (line 729: `double value = std::stod(tokens[3]);`). The stod call in handle_variable_command is not wrapped in try-catch. No mention of CWE-754.

Run 2 did not identify integer underflow in memory-pool block split (lines 128-136: `size_t previous_size = block.size; size_t remaining = previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES;`). No mention of CWE-191 or underflow.

Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion in parser (lines 381-433: parse_expression -> parse_term -> parse_factor -> parse_expression with PAREN_OPEN). No mention of CWE-674 or recursion depth limits.

Run 2 did not identify data race in result cache (lines 440-451: `result_cache_` accessed without `cache_mutex_` in evaluate_cached). The cache_mutex_ member exists but is never used. No mention of CWE-362 in this context.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: TOCTOU Race Condition in File Loading ... Line 544: `if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false;` Line 545: `std::this_thread::sleep_for(std::chrono::milliseconds(25));` Line 546: `std::ifstream file(full_path);` — Race window between existence check and file open, exacerbated by deliberate 25ms sleep.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the TOCTOU race at lines 544-546 with CWE-367 and Medium severity, matching the truth item exactly.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Integer Division by Zero in calculate_route_score ... Line 751: `size_t token_length = (token_end == std::string::npos) ? input.length() : token_end;` Line 752: `return 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length);` — If input is empty string, token_end is npos, token_length becomes input.length() which is 0, causing division by zero.
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the division by zero at lines 751-752 with CWE-369. However, severity was rated Medium instead of Low as specified in the truth item.
